---
title: Statistics
editor:
  render-on-save: true
---

Statistical metrics aggregate numeric properties of nearby features (such as building heights or areas) over the street network, computing summary statistics like `min`, `max`, `mean`, and `var` at each node within specified distance thresholds.

The following examples use the [`layers.compute_stats`](https://cityseer.benchmarkurbanism.com/metrics/layers#compute-stats) method. The same network pre-processing steps can be followed as shown for the network preparation or centrality examples. A dataset with numeric attributes is then needed to compute statistics over the network.

This method requires:

- A `geopandas` `GeoDataFrame` containing the data;
- A `stats_column_labels` parameter identifying the numeric columns for which to compute statistics.

::: {.callout-tip}
These examples assume a prepared network. See [Network Preparation](../networks/index.qmd) for setup.
:::

## Statistics from geopandas data

Calculate building statistics from a `geopandas` `GeoDataFrame`.

[Notebook](gpd_stats.ipynb)

## Statistics from OSM data

Calculate building statistics from `osmnx` data.

[Notebook](osm_stats.ipynb)
